Search the transcript directory only: ./benchmarks/provenance-qa/runs/gpt55-blog-full-matrix-final/fixtures/correction-chain/conditions/subagent-rlm-transcript-search/worktree/memory/transcripts

Question to answer: Why does src/parser/schema.ts trim schema field names after splitting fields instead of before tokenization?

Ignore distractors explicitly: CSV trim cleanup, Network timeout parser.

Use only glob, grep, and read over transcript files. Do not use bash or any mem-mould/session tools. Return compact JSON only with these fields: answer, evidence {session_id, blob_id, message_id}, rationale, irrelevant_context_ignored. The cited message_id must support the full answer, not just a nearby subclaim. If a correction, stale detail, or rejected alternative matters, mention it explicitly in rationale.

**Looking for schema field names**

I’m trying to find the specific field names within a schema. There’s potentially a lot here depending on the complexity of the schema itself. I want to make sure I cover all the relevant fields that might be present. I’ll check any documentation available. Knowing the exact field names will help in organizing the data correctly, ensuring everything fits into the right structure. I can’t wait to get this sorted out!

metadata.preview:
# Schema parser correction chain
session_id: schema_parser_session
title: Schema parser correction chain

## message schema_fact_1
Early note, later superseded: trim the whole schema header before tokenization.

## message schema_fact_2
Correction: tokenize first, then trim each field. This preserves raw delimiter and quoted-field behavior while cleaning field names.

## message schema_fact_3
The earlier before-tokenization rationale was wrong and should not be cited as final.

parts.text: {"answer":"Because tokenizing/splitting first preserves raw delimiter and quoted-field behavior; trimming afterward cleans only the resulting field names.","evidence":{"session_id":"schema_parser_session","blob_id":"schema_parser_session.md","message_id":"schema_fact_2"},"rationale":"schema_fact_2 is the corrected final rationale. The earlier idea to trim before tokenization was explicitly superseded/rejected, so it should not be used.","irrelevant_context_ignored":["CSV trim cleanup","Network timeout parser","correction_chain_decoy_*"]}
